rulings.Case Title: VEDA KOSHES VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS(B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, AUGUST 27, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1, Defendant General Dynamics Corp.¿s motion to dismiss pursuant to C.C.P. 583.310 and 583.360 is granted. No opposition filed. Hearing required unless defendant notifies plaintiff of this tentative ruling and she states that she does not wish to contest. The hearing will be at 9:45a.m. Defendant must bring a proposed order to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: ELVIN GAZZANO VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, AUGUST 27, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 2, Defendant Ric-Wil, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden on the issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff failed to present admissible evidence creating a triable issue whether he could properly identify Ric-Wil pipe or any product or material attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections based upon hearsay to plaintiff¿s declaration where he identified Ric-Wil piping based upon a review of statements in written specifications are sustained. In view of the court¿s sustaining defendant¿s objections, the objections to the declarations of Mr. Ay and Dr. Cohen are sustained as speculative and lacking foundation. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: GARY KASE et al VS. THOMAS DEE ENGINEERING COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, AUGUST 27, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 5, Defendant SB Decking, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Mr. Kase¿s deposition testimony and declaration and Mr. Costigan¿s deposition testimony create a triable issue whether Mr. Kase was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (Selbalith) attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Kase¿s declaration and to Mr. Costigan¿s deposition testimony are overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
